sections.question_synthetic: The sender alleges that mobilehome park activists are engaging in blackmail tactics by threatening to withhold or redirect votes unless elected officials and candidates promise to support rent control ordinances, and requests that the FPPC investigate and take action against these alleged blackmailers and the officials who capitulate to their demands.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming complaint/request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal Q&A sections present. Document dated December 23, 1991 (document header says 1992; letter dated 1991). Heavily annotated with handwritten marks. Writer makes allegations about political blackmail and requests FPPC intervention but does not present a specific legal question seeking formal advice.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender alleges that mobilehome park activists are engaging in blackmail tactics by threatening to withhold or redirect votes unless elected officials and candidates promise to support rent control ordinances, and requests that the FPPC investigate and take action against these alleged blackmailers and the officials who capitulate to their demands.

embedding.summary: Richard H. McManus writes to the FPPC Chairman alleging that mobilehome park activists are blackmailing elected officials and candidates in California by threatening to withhold votes unless they promise to support mobilehome park rent control ordinances. He requests the FPPC investigate and take enforcement action.
